title: Pionandra betacea
taxonomy: Pionandra betacea is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The Sapotaceae family is a large group of flowering plants, commonly known as sapote or mamey sapote, and is closely related to the Ebenaceae and Canellaceae families. These families together are part of the larger order Ericales. The classification of Pionandra betacea is primarily based on its anatomical and morphological characteristics.
